headline: Delta Stock Soars After Earnings. These Airlines Are Climbing Even Higher.
summary: Delta Air Lines  stock was surging 8% on Wednesday.  United Airlines  rose 13%,  Southwest Airlines  jumped 11%, and  Norwegian Cruise Line  was rising 12%.  Delta’s guidance may be a reason it’s lagging behind slightly—if an 8% rise can described in such terms.

headline: Delta Air Lines Stock Surges After Earnings. How It’s Shrugging Off $2 Billion Fuel Hit.
summary: Delta Air Lines  stock was surging ahead of the open Wednesday as the carrier’s earnings and the two-week U.S., Iran cease-fire deal proved to be tailwinds for the shares.  Demand is so strong that Delta expects to post a $1 billion pretax profit in the current quarter despite a more than $2 billion increase in fuel costs.  The carrier is also cutting capacity to mitigate the impact of higher fuel prices, it said.

raw.headline: Delta Air Lines Stock Surges After Earnings. How It’s Shrugging Off $2 Billion Fuel Hit.
raw.summary: Delta Air Lines  stock was surging ahead of the open Wednesday as the carrier’s earnings and the two-week U.S., Iran cease-fire deal proved to be tailwinds for the shares.  Demand is so strong that Delta expects to post a $1 billion pretax profit in the current quarter despite a more than $2 billion increase in fuel costs.  The carrier is also cutting capacity to mitigate the impact of higher fuel prices, it said.

headline: Here Are the Stocks Rising the Most (and Some Losers)
summary: The market is rallying on hopes of a resumption of energy flows from the Middle East. Here are a few of the stocks moving the most as some of the big moves of recent weeks start to unwind. Going up: ✈️ Shares in Delta Air Lines (DAL), United Airlines (UAL) and Southwest Airlines (LUV), which have been walloped by higher fuel prices, are surging premarket.

headline: Shares of airline companies are trading higher in response to President Trump's social media post indicating that the U.S will work closely with Iran and that many of the 15 points in the proposed plan to end the war have been agreed to. The industry may also be experiencing strength in response to Delta Air Lines' Q1 sales beat.
